title: Neomammillaria herrerae
taxonomy: Neomammillaria herrerae is classified within the family Cactaceae. This classification is due to its cactus-like characteristics and growth patterns. Neomammillaria herrerae further belongs to the subfamily Cactoideae, which comprises the majority of cacti species. Within the subfamily, it is part of the tribe Cacteae, characterized by its unique features and adaptations. Additionally, Neomammillaria herrerae is placed within the genus Neomammillaria, which is closely related to the genus Mammillaria.

intro: Neomammillaria herrerae, also known as the Herrera's pincushion cactus, is a type of succulent plant belonging to the family Cactaceae. This plant has been traditionally used in medicine for its potential health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, making it useful for treating various conditions such as wounds, infections, and oxidative stress. The bioactive constituents of Neomammillaria herrerae include flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made from this plant include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, often used to treat skin conditions, digestive issues, and respiratory problems.
